heading: Registration and information management
label: 1881 Registration and information management

Amendments2018—Subsec. (g)(4), (5). , added par. (4) and redesignated former par. (4) as (5).Pub. L. 115–405, § 202(1)Subsec. (h). , added subsec. (h).Pub. L. 115–405, § 202(2)2007—Subsec. (g). added subsec. (g) and struck out former subsec. (g) which required a report to Congress.Pub. L. 109–479

Change of NameCommittee on Resources of House of Representatives changed to Committee on Natural Resources of House of Representatives by House Resolution No. 6, One Hundred Tenth Congress, .Jan. 5, 2007

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of authorities, functions, personnel, and assets of the Coast Guard, including the authorities and functions of the Secretary of Transportation relating thereto, to the Department of Homeland Security, and for treatment of related references, see sections 468(b), 551(d), 552(d), and 557 of Title 6, Domestic Security, and the Department of Homeland Security Reorganization Plan of , as modified, set out as a note under .November 25, 2002section 542 of Title 6

NAS Report Recommendations, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–405, § 201(b)132 Stat. 5358Dec. 31, 2018“The Secretary of Commerce shall take into consideration and, to the extent feasible, implement the recommendations of the National Academy of Sciences in the report entitled ‘Review of the Marine Recreational Information Program (2017)’, and shall submit, every 2 years following the date of enactment of this Act [], a report to the appropriate committees of Congress [Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives] detailing progress made implementing those recommendations. Recommendations considered shall include—Dec. 31, 2018“(1)prioritizing the evaluation of electronic data collection, including smartphone applications, electronic diaries for prospective data collection, and an internet website option for panel members or for the public;“(2)evaluating whether the design of the Marine Recreational Information Program for the purposes of stock assessment and the determination of stock management reference points is compatible with the needs of in-season management of annual catch limits; and“(3)if the Marine Recreational Information Program is incompatible with the needs of in-season management of annual catch limits, determining an alternative method for in-season management.”

children.heading: Standardized fishing vessel registration and information management system
children.label: a Standardized fishing vessel registration and information management system

children.text: be designed to standardize the requirements of vessel registration and information collection systems required by this chapter, the Marine Mammal Protection Act ( et seq.), and any other marine resource law implemented by the Secretary, and, with the permission of a State, any marine resource law implemented by such State;16 U.S.C. 1361
integrate information collection programs under existing fishery management plans into a non-duplicative information collection and management system;
avoid duplication of existing State, tribal, or Federal systems and shall utilize, to the maximum extent practicable, information collected from existing systems;
provide for implementation of the system through cooperative agreements with appropriate State, regional, or tribal entities and Marine Fisheries Commissions;
provide for funding (subject to appropriations) to assist appropriate State, regional, or tribal entities and Marine Fisheries Commissions in implementation;
establish standardized units of measurement, nomenclature, and formats for the collection and submission of information;
minimize the paperwork required for vessels registered under the system;
include all species of fish within the geographic areas of authority of the Councils and all fishing vessels including charter fishing vessels, but excluding recreational fishing vessels;

require United States fish processors, and fish dealers and other first ex-vessel purchasers of fish that are subject to the proposed system, to submit information (other than economic information) which may be necessary to meet the goals of the proposed system; and

children.label: A
children.text: the confidentiality of information collected under this section in accordance with ; andsection 1881a(b) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the timely release or availability to the public of information collected under this section consistent with .section 1881a(b) of this title
the name and official number or other identification, together with the name and address of the owner or operator or both;

gross tonnage, vessel capacity, type and quantity of fishing gear, mode of operation (catcher, catcher processor, or other), and such other pertinent information with respect to vessel characteristics as the Secretary may require; and
identification (by species, gear type, geographic area of operations, and season) of the fisheries in which the fishing vessel participates.
the number of vessels participating in the fishery including charter fishing vessels;
the time period in which the fishery occurs;
the approximate geographic location or official reporting area where the fishery occurs;
a description of fishing gear used in the fishery, including the amount and type of such gear and the appropriate unit of fishing effort; and
other information required under subsection 1853(a)(5) of this title or requested by the Council under .1section 1881a of this titleSo in original. Probably should be “section”.1

children.heading: Use of registration
children.label: d Use of registration
children.text: Any registration recommended under this section shall not be considered a permit for the purposes of this chapter, and the Secretary may not propose to revoke, suspend, deny, or impose any other conditions or restrictions on any such registration or the use of such registration under this chapter.

a description of the arrangements of the Secretary for consultation and cooperation with the department in which the Coast Guard is operating, the States, the Councils, Marine Fisheries Commissions, the fishing industry and other interested parties; and
any proposed regulations or legislation necessary to implement the proposal.
any modifications made after comment and consultation;
a proposed implementation schedule, including a schedule for the proposed cooperative agreements required under subsection (a)(4); and
recommendations for any such additional legislation as the Secretary considers necessary or desirable to implement the proposed system.

children.label: i
children.text: in the Exclusive Economic Zone;

children.label: ii
children.text: for anadromous species; or

children.label: iii
children.text: for Continental Shelf fishery resources beyond the Exclusive Economic Zone; and

children.label: B
children.text: if appropriate, the registration (including the ownership, operator, and identification of the vessel) of vessels used in such fishing.

children.heading: State programs
children.label: 2 State programs
children.text: The Secretary shall exempt from registration under the program recreational fishermen and charter fishing vessels licensed, permitted, or registered under the laws of a State if the Secretary determines that information from the State program is suitable for the Secretary’s use or is used to assist in completing marine recreational fisheries statistical surveys, or evaluating the effects of proposed conservation and management measures for marine recreational fisheries.

children.heading: Improvement of the marine recreational fishery statistics survey
children.label: A Improvement of the marine recreational fishery statistics survey
children.text: Within 24 months after , the Secretary, in consultation with representatives of the recreational fishing industry and experts in statistics, technology, and other appropriate fields, shall establish a program to improve the quality and accuracy of information generated by the Marine Recreational Fishery Statistics Survey, with a goal of achieving acceptable accuracy and utility for each individual fishery.January 12, 2007

children.label: i
children.text: redesigning the Survey to improve the effectiveness and appropriateness of sampling and estimation procedures, its applicability to various kinds of management decisions, and its usefulness for social and economic analyses; and

children.label: ii
children.text: providing for ongoing technical evaluation and modification as needed to meet emerging management needs.

children.label: i
children.text: an adequate number of intercepts to accurately estimate recreational catch and effort;

children.label: ii
children.text: use of surveys that target anglers registered or licensed at the State or Federal level to collect participation and effort data;

children.label: iii
children.text: collection and analysis of vessel trip report data from charter fishing vessels;

children.label: iv
children.text: development of a weather corrective factor that can be applied to recreational catch and effort estimates; and

children.label: v
children.text: an independent committee composed of recreational fishermen, academics, persons with expertise in stock assessments and survey design, and appropriate personnel from the National Marine Fisheries Service to review the collection estimates, geographic, and other variables related to dockside intercepts and to identify deficiencies in recreational data collection, and possible correction measures.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: D Deadline
children.text: The Secretary shall complete the program under this paragraph and implement the improved Marine Recreational Fishery Statistics Survey not later than .January 1, 2009

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a partnership with a State to develop best practices for implementing the State program established under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Guidance
children.label: B Guidance
children.text: The Secretary shall develop guidance, in cooperation with the States, that details best practices for administering State programs pursuant to paragraph (2), and provide such guidance to the States.

children.label: I
children.text: the information provided under subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (1) for each registry program established under that paragraph; and

children.label: II
children.text: the information from each State program that is used to assist in completing surveys or evaluating effects of conservation and management measures under paragraph (2);

children.label: ii
children.text: priorities for improving recreational fishing data collection; and

children.label: iii
children.text: an explanation of any use of information collected by such State programs and by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: improve implementation of State programs consistent with this subsection; and

children.label: II
children.text: assist such programs in complying with requirements related to changes in recreational data collection under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: ii Use of funds
children.text: Any funds awarded through such grants shall be used to support data collection, quality assurance, and outreach to entities submitting such data. The Secretary shall prioritize such grants based on the ability of the grant to improve the quality and accuracy of such programs.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 5 Report
children.text: Within 24 months after establishment of the program, the Secretary shall submit a report to Congress that describes the progress made toward achieving the goals and objectives of the program.

children.label: A
children.text: how the design of the Marine Recreational Information Program, for the purposes of stock assessment and the determination of stock management reference points, can be improved to better meet the needs of in-season management of annual catch limits under ; andsection 1853(a)(15) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: what actions the Secretary, Councils, and States could take to improve the accuracy and timeliness of data collection and analysis to improve the Marine Recreational Information Program and facilitate in-season management; and

children.label: A
children.text: changes to be made to the Marine Recreational Information Program to make the program better meet the needs of in-season management of annual catch limits and other requirements under such section; and

children.label: B
children.text: alternative management approaches that could be applied to recreational fisheries for which the Marine Recreational Information Program is not meeting the needs of in-season management of annual catch limits, consistent with other requirements of this chapter, until such time as the changes in subparagraph (A) are implemented.
